title: Strange metallicity in the Kagome metal Ni$_3$In: a DMFT investigation
authors: Ruslan Mushkaev, Francesco Petocchi, Philipp Werner
abstract: Strange metallicity, characterized by a linear temperature dependence of the resistivity, is observed in a broad range of correlated materials, including heavy-fermion compounds and cuprate superconductors. It has also recently been reported for the Kagome metal Ni$_3$In, where almost localized and itinerant electronic degrees of freedom coexist as a result of a partially flat band. We investigate the correlated electronic structure and transport properties of Ni$_3$In with dynamical mean field theory (DMFT) calculations performed on a minimal single-band Hubbard model, constructed from compact molecular orbitals. Despite the large band filling, even for moderate Hubbard repulsion, we observe a non-Fermi-liquid like frequency dependence of the self-energy, as well as the formation of local magnetic moments. With increased hole doping, a crossover to a heavy Fermi-liquid regime is found. We interpret these results in terms of an effective model for the partially filled narrow band near $k_z=0$.
summary: This paper uses DMFT to show that the strange metallicity in the Kagome metal Ni3In arises from a partially filled quasi-flat band. The researchers demonstrate that a single-band Hubbard model can capture the non-Fermi-liquid behavior and the crossover to a heavy Fermi-liquid regime upon hole doping. This provides a microscopic link between the Kagome lattice geometry and anomalous transport properties.
main_problem: Investigating the microscopic origins of strange metallicity and the coexistence of localized and itinerant electronic degrees of freedom in the Kagome metal Ni3In.
main_result: The study finds that a single-band Hubbard model can reproduce the linear-in-T resistivity and non-Fermi-liquid behavior, which is driven by the presence of a partially filled quasi-flat band.
method: Dynamical Mean Field Theory (DMFT) using a hybridization-expansion continuous-time Monte Carlo (CT-HYB) solver applied to a Hubbard model derived from DFT-based Wannier functions.
model_or_system: The physical system is the Kagome metal Ni3In. The theoretical model is a minimal single-band Hubbard model on a stacked triangular lattice, constructed from Compact Molecular Orbitals (CMOs) to capture the electronic structure near the Fermi level.
key_observables: Temperature-dependent resistivity, imaginary part of the self-energy, local density of states (DOS), and static local spin susceptibility.
important_parameters: Hubbard repulsion U (1-2 eV), band filling (n approx 1.85), and temperature (32-193 K).
main_assumptions: The resistivity calculation neglects vertex corrections within the DMFT approximation; the model is reduced to a minimal single-band Hubbard model.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the Ni Kagome layer and orbital axes; Figure 2 displays DFT bandstructure and DOS; Figure 10-11 illustrate the stacked triangular lattice model and orbital character of the CMO band; Figure 12 provides a numerical benchmark for resistivity.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of strange metallicity in Ni3In, describes the construction of the Hubbard model from DFT/Wannier functions, presents DMFT results regarding self-energy and resistivity, explores the effects of hole doping, and concludes with a discussion on the role of the quasi-flat band.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1: Panel (a): one of the Ni3In Kagome layers. Panel (b): shifted Ni3In unit cell. Panel (c): local orbital axes.

FIG. 2: Left panel: DFT bandstructure (black) and 6 Wannier bands (orange), derived from a Ni-dxz Wannier Hamiltonian. The band derived from the CMO states is the blue dashed line. Middle and right panels: Full DOS for the 6-band and 1-band (CMO) models, and DOS calculated in the kz = 0 plane.
FIG. 3: Drude fit of the current-current correlation func- tion for (a) β =60 and (b) 240 eV−1 (193 K and 48 K respectively).
FIG. 4: DMFT local density of states per spin for the one-band model of Ni3In for U = 1 eV and 2 eV, at β = 100 eV−1 (116 K). The black curve is the local DOS of the noninteracting model.
FIG. 5: Local DMFT self-energies for Ni3In for different interactions and temperatures in the undoped case (top row) and for different doping values δ = 1.85 −n at U = 1 eV (second and third rows).
